paper_title: Proteomic Analysis of a Sea-Ice Diatom: Salinity Acclimation Provides New Insight into the Dimethylsulfoniopropionate Production Pathway

methods.method_name: 2DE and Image Analysis of T2 Proteins
methods.description: This method describes the process of two-dimensional electrophoresis (2DE) and subsequent image analysis of T2 proteins to study protein expression and modifications under different conditions.

steps.description: Equal amounts of protein (150 mg per sample) were brought up to a final volume of 300 mL rehydration buffer amended with 60 mM dithiothreitol (DTT), 0.2% biolytes pH 3 to 10 (Bio-Rad), and 280 units of benzonase (Sigma-Aldrich).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare protein samples with rehydration buffer and additives

steps.description: Immobilized pH 4 to 7 gradient strips (17 cm, Bio-Rad) were rehydrated with the sample by passive overnight rehydration.
steps.searchable_summary: rehydrate pH gradient strips with protein samples overnight

steps.description: Isoelectric focusing was performed in PROTEAN IEF cells (Bio-Rad) with a maximum current of 50 mA/strip.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isoelectric focusing on rehydrated strips

steps.description: Strips were actively desalted at 50 V for 1.5 h, followed by linear increase to a maximum of 8,000 V over 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: desalt and increase voltage on protein strips

steps.description: Wicks were then changed and focusing was completed by increasing voltage linearly to 10,000 V for 1 h, followed by a rapid increase to 10,000 V for 55,000 V h.
steps.searchable_summary: complete focusing by increasing voltage on protein strips

steps.description: Focused strips were then frozen at 280°C.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze focused protein strips

steps.description: Prior to second dimension separation, proteins were sequentially reduced and alkylated in equilibration buffer containing 6 M urea, 0.375 M Tris pH 8.8, 2% SDS, and 20% glycerol.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce and alkylate proteins in equilibration buffer

steps.description: For reducing, thawed strips were gently rocked for 30 min in equilibration buffer with 2.5% DTT.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce proteins with DTT in equilibration buffer

steps.description: Reducing buffer was aspirated off and strips were then alkylated by gently rocking for another 30 min in the dark with equilibration buffer plus 3% iodoacetamide (IAA).
steps.searchable_summary: alkylate proteins with IAA in equilibration buffer

steps.description: Strips were loaded on 12% polyacrylamide gels approximately 1 cm to the right of a wick soaked in 10 mL of precision plus protein unstained standards (Bio-Rad) and overlaid with bromophenol blue tinted agarose.
steps.searchable_summary: load protein strips on polyacrylamide gels

steps.description: SDS-PAGE separation was performed in Protean II z cell system units maintained at 15°C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SDS-PAGE separation on protein gels

steps.description: Gels were electrophoresed in 25 mM Tris, 190 mM Gly, 0.1% SDS running buffer at 6 mA for 10 min, 16 mA for 30 min, and 24 mA for approximately 4 h (per gel) until the bromophenol blue front was roughly 1 cm from bottom of gel.
steps.searchable_summary: electrophorese protein gels with running buffer

steps.description: Gels were fixed with 10% methanol/ 7% acetic acid, then stained overnight with Sypro Ruby (Invitrogen), rinsed with water, destained with 10% methanol/7% acetic acid, washed with water, and imaged on an FX pro plus fluorescent imager (Bio-Rad).
steps.searchable_summary: fix, stain, and image protein gels

steps.description: Control and treatment pairs were always run together to evenly distribute any variability in gel running conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: run control and treatment pairs together

steps.description: Two to three technical replicates were run per sample and the best gel selected for analysis using PDQuest software, version 7.1 (Bio-Rad).
steps.searchable_summary: select best gel for analysis using PDQuest

steps.description: Spots were detected automatically and matched across the 10 sample gels.
steps.searchable_summary: detect and match spots across sample gels

steps.description: The matchset was manually edited in appropriate magnification and contrast settings to confirm and improve matches.
steps.searchable_summary: manually edit matchset for confirmation

steps.description: Novel spots were added to master and other gels to enable comparison across gels.
steps.searchable_summary: add novel spots for comparison

steps.description: Relative spot intensity (cumulative pixel intensity) was normalized to total intensity of all valid spots on each gel.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize spot intensity

steps.description: A quantity table for all spots on all gels was exported to a spreadsheet for statistical analysis using MATLAB (version 2010, Mathworks, Inc.).
steps.searchable_summary: export quantity table for statistical analysis

steps.description: P values for log-normalized spot intensities were calculated using the mattest function to perform two-sample t tests with permutations.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate p values using two-sample t tests

steps.description: The mafdr function was then used to calculate q values that correct for FDR inherent in multiple comparisons within large transcriptomic and proteomic datasets.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate q values to correct for FDR

methods.description: This method involves screening potential DMSP pathway genes for subcellular localization presequences to determine their targeting within the cell.

steps.description: Potential DMSP pathway genes identified from 2DE gels were screened for subcellular localization presequences.
steps.searchable_summary: screen genes for subcellular localization presequences

steps.description: Alternative N-terminal extensions, along with blast protein matches from the other two sequenced diatoms, were also screened for the presence of chloroplast bipartite targeting signals.
steps.searchable_summary: screen for chloroplast bipartite targeting signals

steps.description: Signal peptides and cleavage site predictions were performed by SignalP 3.0 and manually evaluated for the conserved diatom chloroplast motif ASAFAP at the signal peptide cleavage site.
steps.searchable_summary: predict and evaluate signal peptides and cleavage sites

steps.description: Plastid transit peptide domains were identified by TargetP.
steps.searchable_summary: identify plastid transit peptide domains

steps.description: Proteins were classified as plastid targeted if they possess a signal peptide and no endoplasmic reticulum retention signal, possess some transit peptide features beyond the N-terminal signal peptide, and contain one of the bulky hydrophobic amino acids in the +1 position of the signal peptide cleavage site.
steps.searchable_summary: classify proteins as plastid targeted
